title: Company and development vertical acceptance plan defined
objective: Define an end-to-end acceptance trace proving that one authorized company goal becomes governed development work, a verified proof on canonical PostgreSQL state, a provenance-bearing release, an observed operating outcome, and a learned next action — with no paper completion and no external company, repository, or production mutation during planning. The acceptance flow is the governed intent-to-proof execution spine (LIFEOS-003) instantiated for the company/development vertical (COMPANY-001, DEVELOP-001) and closed with proof-derived learning (LIFEOS-005), landing every stage in the STORE-001 tiered authority model.

sources.context_matrix: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv

input_families.COMPANY-001: company governance/authority — every company object has owner, authority path, bounded decision, approval, evidence, rollback/non-action rule, no agent self-authorization
input_families.DEVELOP-001: development build/release family — source ownership, isolated work, local-first verification, CI receipts, GitHub hygiene, release provenance, installed proof, rollback, capability learning
input_families.LIFEOS-003: governed intent-to-proof execution spine — identity, policy, task graph, simulation, hermetic execution, proof, rollback, human-approval gates, next-action derivation, proof-bearing on canonical PostgreSQL state
input_families.LIFEOS-005: intelligence substrate — proof-derived learning, promotion of proven state only, SONA next-action

acceptance_flow_summary: authority -> task graph -> implementation -> proof -> release -> outcome -> rollback -> learning, under two cross-cutting invariants (no paper completion; no external mutation during planning). Each of the eight stages is assigned exactly once to a single owner and a single store-authority tier drawn from STORE-001; PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single durable writer of every macro-state stage, redb holds only transient stage state, envctl is the forward-only bridge and projection engine, nu_plugin CodeDB is the ingestion path, and AgentDB (.rvf) holds non-authoritative learning.

work_items.surface: Company goal authorization under David's final authority (COMPANY-001 authority graph; CECCA single internal CEO-agent per DECIDE-006)
work_items.owner: David (final authority) via LifeOS Governance Agent / CECCA
work_items.decision: A company goal enters the acceptance flow only as an authority record signed under David's final authority; CECCA is the single internal CEO-agent boundary and no agent self-authorizes (COMPANY-001 gate). The authority record is durable relational macro-state.
work_items.store_authority: durable relational macro-state (identity, permission, provenance) -> PostgreSQL + RuVector, single durable writer (STORE-001 class 1)
work_items.sync_edge: authority row written directly to PostgreSQL; no redb durable copy
work_items.evidence_kind: signed authority + provenance row in PostgreSQL; the goal is not 'authorized' without David's sign-off
work_items.rollback: revoke the authority record; no downstream stage proceeds; company state unchanged

work_items.surface: Decomposition of the authorized goal into a bounded, proof-bearing governed task graph (LIFEOS-003 execution spine)
work_items.decision: The authorized goal is decomposed into bounded task rows with parent/child and causal dependency edges (identity -> policy -> task graph -> simulation gate); each child is bounded and proof-bearing, no unlimited agent authority. Simulation is required before any execution child runs.
work_items.store_authority: task rows -> PostgreSQL relational macro-state (STORE-001 class 1); causal dependency edges -> PostgreSQL + RuVector GNN semantic/graph class (STORE-001 class 2, native No-Sidecar extension)
work_items.sync_edge: task-graph rows and causal edges written to PostgreSQL; RuVector holds the GNN edges natively in-database
work_items.recovery: PostgreSQL PITR + reproducible re-derivation of causal edges
work_items.evidence_kind: task-graph rows with bounded scope + causal edges in PostgreSQL; simulation_required = TRUE gate present on every execution child
work_items.rollback: restore the prior task graph; no execution-plane source is changed (LIFEOS-003 rollback)

work_items.surface: Bounded development work item implemented as isolated work with local-first verification (DEVELOP-001)
work_items.decision: Implementation happens as isolated work in a Copy-On-Write staging branch inside PostgreSQL; code enters through the ingestion path Nushell parser -> nu_plugin CodeDB -> redb buffer -> envctl embed bridge -> PostgreSQL; agent thoughts and terminal traces during the edit are transient in redb; the canonical code owner is PostgreSQL and the staged rows merge to the main trunk only after green local-first verification.
work_items.store_authority: code AST/rows -> PostgreSQL canonical (STORE-001 class 1); redb = transient high-frequency buffer/scratchpad for the edit's thoughts/traces (STORE-001 class 3); nu_plugin CodeDB = parser/ingestion path; envctl = forward-only redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL bridge
work_items.sync_edge: forward-only redb -> envctl(embed via all-MiniLM-L6-v2) -> PostgreSQL; COW staging branch isolates the edit inside PostgreSQL; no dual-write
work_items.recovery: discard the COW staging branch; redb transient state is transient by definition; PostgreSQL PITR for merged rows
work_items.evidence_kind: staged code rows + local-first verification receipt (bun run test / cargo test --workspace --locked green) before merge
work_items.rollback: discard the COW staging branch; no repository or main-trunk mutation

work_items.surface: Proof record produced on canonical PostgreSQL state and bound to a Cryptographic Witness Chain (LIFEOS-003 proof-bearing; blueprint anti-bluffing)
work_items.decision: A proof record carrying the edit's SHA-256 content identity is produced on canonical PostgreSQL state (single durable writer per STORE-001); the code change is bound to a SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chain rooted in the source vector so the claim cannot be bluffed; RuVector MinCut severs any agent that drifts. Status 'pass' requires a populated gate_result, never a self-declared completion.
work_items.store_authority: durable provenance/proof -> PostgreSQL canonical (STORE-001 class 1); witness chain = SHAKE256 rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors
work_items.sync_edge: proof + witness chain committed to PostgreSQL; MinCut/Causal-Graph verification gate before acceptance
work_items.evidence_kind: proof-record row + SHAKE256 witness chain + CI receipt; gate_result with concrete per-clause values
work_items.rollback: mark the proof failed; MinCut isolates the offending agent; no promotion without a green proof

work_items.surface: Provenance-bearing release via envctl projection and Nix musl static build (DEVELOP-001 release provenance)
work_items.owner: LifeOS Development Agent + Yazelix/Nix build (release phase)
work_items.decision: A release is produced by the envctl projection engine (query PostgreSQL production branch -> module_path directory mapping -> concatenate raw_code blocks -> dump clean tree) then compiled with Nix against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl for an install-in-place static binary; the release manifest carries the SHA-256 / source-commit-and-PR lineage; the projected tree is derived and disposable and is built only from a proven branch.
work_items.store_authority: derived projection (materialized source dir, release tree) -> envctl read-only projection FROM PostgreSQL (STORE-001 class 5, derived/disposable); PostgreSQL remains the source of truth
work_items.recovery: re-projection from PostgreSQL via module_path metadata (deterministic)
work_items.evidence_kind: release provenance manifest (source commit/PR, SHA-256 identity, musl target); release only from a green-proof branch
work_items.rollback: discard the projected tree and release artifact; re-project the prior production branch; no published or production release occurs during planning

work_items.surface: Observed operating outcome / installed proof from the released capability (DEVELOP-001 installed proof; LIFEOS-003 execution outcome)
work_items.owner: LifeOS Governance / QA Agent + Runtime Kernel
work_items.decision: The released capability runs and produces a measured operating outcome; high-frequency operating traces and telemetry buffer in redb as an ultra-fast scratchpad / application-level WAL, and envctl flushes only settled, significant outcome states forward into PostgreSQL; a concrete runtime signal (for example a db_health status / applied-migration count) is the recorded observed outcome.
work_items.store_authority: transient operating traces/telemetry -> redb (STORE-001 class 3); settled significant outcome -> PostgreSQL canonical via envctl forward flush (STORE-001 class 3 sync edge)
work_items.sync_edge: redb -> envctl flush -> PostgreSQL (only significant states persist); LifeOS UI reads live redb via UDS for real-time status
work_items.recovery: redb local disk for live telemetry (transient by definition); PostgreSQL PITR for the settled outcome row
work_items.evidence_kind: observed-outcome row in PostgreSQL + runtime signal (e.g. db_health status='healthy', applied_migrations == embedded count)
work_items.rollback: transient telemetry expires; if the outcome regresses, PostgreSQL PITR restore; no production mutation during planning

work_items.surface: End-to-end rollback contract leaving company, repository, and production state unchanged (COMPANY-002 rollback_plan)
work_items.decision: Any stage failure triggers a defined rollback: COW staging-branch discard (implementation), PostgreSQL PITR restore (durable macro-state), re-projection (release), and archived-plan restore (planning artifacts). No class leaves redb-durable except by a green live BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover (STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant); there is never an unverified swap or a dual-write.
work_items.store_authority: recovery sources per STORE-001 — PostgreSQL PITR (durable classes 1/2), redb local disk (transient class 3), envctl re-projection (derived class 5), archived acceptance plan (planning artifacts)
work_items.sync_edge: no forward promotion on a failed stage; rollback is directional-reverse only within the owning tier; no dual-write introduced
work_items.recovery: restore the archived acceptance plan; PITR for durable state; re-projection for derived state
work_items.evidence_kind: a named rollback command per stage; zero unarchived destructive actions (PRESERVE-001 discipline)
work_items.rollback: restore the archived acceptance plan itself; company, repository, and production state remain unchanged

work_items.surface: Learned next action promoted only from proven state (LIFEOS-005 proof-derived learning; SONA; LIFEOS-003 next-action derivation)
work_items.decision: The proven outcome updates per-agent cognition (SONA policy matrix / MicroLoRA success threshold) in AgentDB (.rvf) non-authoritatively; the derived next-action is promoted into the canonical PostgreSQL task graph ONLY from proven state (LIFEOS-005 'promotion of proven state only'), and the promotion is witnessed by a SHAKE256 chain rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors. A failed or unproven stage promotes no learning.
work_items.store_authority: per-agent cognition (SONA/MicroLoRA) -> AgentDB (.rvf), non-authoritative (STORE-001 class 4); promoted next-action row -> PostgreSQL canonical (STORE-001 class 1), only from proven state
work_items.sync_edge: AgentDB self-contained; provenance links back to the PostgreSQL knowledge graph; the promoted next-action is a forward write to PostgreSQL gated on a green proof
work_items.recovery: AgentDB .rvf file (regenerable from base model + training history); PostgreSQL PITR for the promoted next-action row
work_items.evidence_kind: next_action row derived from a green proof (traceable to COMPANY-002-D); no next_action promoted from a failed/unproven stage
work_items.rollback: revert the promoted next-action row; AgentDB cognition is regenerable; the base model and knowledge graph are unchanged

work_items.gate_clause: no paper completion (cross-cutting invariant over A-H)
work_items.surface: Every stage transitions to done/pass only against concrete, recoverable evidence
work_items.decision: No stage self-declares completion. A stage is 'done' only with concrete evidence: a signed authority row (A), bounded task rows + causal edges (B), a green local-first verification receipt (C), a proof-record row with a populated gate_result + SHAKE256 witness chain (D), a provenance manifest (E), an observed-outcome row (F), a named rollback command (G), and a proof-derived next-action (H). This enforces the AGENTS.md rule 'never mark done without completion evidence' and STORE-001's 'verified cutover, never by assumption'.
work_items.store_authority: invariant asserted over all PostgreSQL-canonical stage evidence; no new store owner introduced
work_items.evidence_kind: each stage in A-H names its own evidence_kind; this item asserts the union is mandatory
work_items.rollback: any stage lacking evidence is treated as not-done and cannot advance the flow

work_items.gate_clause: no external mutation during planning (cross-cutting invariant over A-H)
work_items.surface: The entire acceptance flow is a proof-cell simulation; no company, repository, or production state is mutated
work_items.decision: COMPANY-002 runs in the proof-cell with simulation_required = TRUE. blocked_paths are honored: organizational action, production deployment, release, product implementation, and secrets/** are all off-limits. The external-secret / provenance authority class stays in the OS keyring + committed pins, unchanged (STORE-001 class 6). No PostgreSQL, redb, AgentDB, repository, CI, or production runtime is written; product_execution_performed is false.
work_items.store_authority: external-secret / provenance authority -> OS keyring + committed source pins, unchanged (STORE-001 class 6); no live tier is written
work_items.recovery: restore the archived acceptance plan; live state was never touched
work_items.evidence_kind: allowed_paths limited to planning-spine-v0/generated/** and planning-spine-v0/proof_records/**; only two JSON planning files are written
work_items.rollback: restore the archived acceptance plan; company, repository, and production state remain unchanged

reference_instance.note: A concrete, fully grounded instantiation of the eight-stage flow using the already-proven CodeDB BlobStore PostgreSQL parity cutover, so the plan is a real contract rather than a template. Every element traces to STORE-001, ADOPT-001, or the Architecture Blueprint. This instance is described for traceability only; it is not executed here.
reference_instance.company_goal: Cut over the CodeDB source-blob durable class from redb to PostgreSQL under one SHA-256 content identity (STORE-001 migration_path for durable relational macro-state).
reference_instance.A_authority: STORE-001 owner decision 'adopt-intended-tiered-architecture' (owner: David / LifeOS Data Agent / Runtime Kernel) authorizes the cutover; no agent self-authorizes.
reference_instance.B_task_graph: STORE-001 unblocks PGAUTH-001..006 and POSTGRES-001 as the bounded governed decomposition; causal dependency edges recorded PostgreSQL-side.
reference_instance.C_implementation: codedb_store_pg::PgStore already implements the BlobStore trait (ADOPT-001 'PgStore (PostgreSQL parity)') as isolated work; ingestion path buffers parsed AST blocks into redb before the envctl bridge.
reference_instance.D_proof: blobstore_parity.rs proves SHA-256 symmetry across backends (ADOPT-001 'SHA-256 identity'); the green live parity run against a disposable PostgreSQL is the durable-class cutover proof (STORE-001 open dependency, CDB086/CDB106-AC05 lane).
reference_instance.E_release: envctl export contract materializes exact-byte source (ADOPT-001 'envctl export contract' + 'Exact-byte materialization') into a clean tree for Nix musl static compilation.
reference_instance.F_outcome: db_health returns status + applied_migrations against the PostgreSQL-backed store as the observed operating outcome (per the storage-layer contract in CLAUDE.md); live telemetry buffers in redb, settled state flushes to PostgreSQL.
reference_instance.G_rollback: the durable class stays on redb until the parity run is green (STORE-001 invariant 'a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run'); no unverified swap.
reference_instance.H_learning: only after this class's proof is green does the next class cut over ('one verified swap at a time'); SONA updates the agent's success threshold non-authoritatively in AgentDB.

acceptance_gate: The acceptance plan passes when all eight flow stages (A-H) are each assigned exactly once to a single owner and a single STORE-001 store-authority tier with a defined sync edge, recovery source, evidence kind, and rollback; the two cross-cutting invariants (I no paper completion, J no external mutation) hold over A-H; PostgreSQL + RuVector is the sole durable writer of every macro-state stage; redb, envctl, nu_plugin CodeDB, and AgentDB keep their intended non-durable roles; and COMPANY-002.proof.json records zero external company, repository, or production mutation with product_execution_performed = false.
store_001_alignment: Store-aligned, not store-neutral: the acceptance flow lands each stage in exactly one STORE-001 class — authority/task-graph/proof/promoted-learning on PostgreSQL canonical (classes 1/2), implementation edit-thoughts and operating telemetry on redb transient (class 3), non-authoritative cognition on AgentDB (class 4), release projection derived from PostgreSQL via envctl (class 5), and secrets untouched in the keyring (class 6). No stage adds a second durable writer, no stage reclassifies a class silently, and the reference-instance cutover moves off redb-durable only via the verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract.
rollback_plan: Restore the archived acceptance plan; company, repository, and production state remain unchanged.
next_action: Decompose the smallest company-to-development proof (the reference-instance cutover) after both COMPANY-001 and DEVELOP-001 task families are bounded.
